ASSISTANTMESSAGE #58
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/activity-duration.spec.js",
  "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-209\n * Verifies:\n *  1. AI decision cycle re-fires after 30 real seconds (second cycle)\n *  2. Characters remain at objects for the expected activity duration\n *  3. Object timers (stove, bonsai, etc.) respect configured durations\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n\ntest.describe('Regression: Activity duration and 30-second AI cycle', () => {\n  test('AI decision cycle re-fires after ~30s \u2014 second wave of LLM requests', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * Each character should fire exactly one LLM request per 30-second cycle.\n     * After ~65s we expect: 6 total requests (2 per character \u00d7 3 characters).\n     * This verifies the rescheduling / timer logic after the first decision fires.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n\n    const byChar = new Map();\n\n    page.on('request', req => {\n      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {\n        try {\n          const body = JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}');\n          const match = (body.system || '').match(/You are (Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);\n          if (match) {\n            const name = match[1];\n            byChar.set(name, (byChar.get(name) || 0) + 1);\n          }\n        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n      }\n    });\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Wait for two full 30-second cycles (with 5s margin)\n    await page.waitForTimeout(65_000);\n\n    // Each of the 3 characters should have fired at least twice\n    expect(byChar.size, 'All 3 characters must have sent requests').toBe(3);\n    for (const [name, count] of byChar) {\n      expect(\n        count,\n        `${name} sent ${count} request(s) \u2014 expected \u22652 across 65s (two 30s cycles)`\n      ).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);\n    }\n\n    console.log('LLM request counts per character:', Object.fromEntries(byChar));\n  });\n\n  test('stove object stays in active state for ~30 seconds before returning to idle', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * The stove's activity duration is 30 real seconds.\n     * Trigger it via the engine and verify it doesn't auto-reset prematurely.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Manually trigger the stove via the engine\n    await page.evaluate(() => {\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('stove', 'use');\n    });\n\n    // Verify stove transitioned to an active state (not idle)\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    const activated = await getEngineState(page);\n    const stove = activated.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'stove');\n    expect(stove?.state, 'Stove should enter active state after interact').not.toBe('idle');\n    console.log(`Stove state after trigger: ${stove?.state}`);\n\n    // Check at 20s \u2014 stove must still be active (not yet auto-reset)\n    await page.waitForTimeout(20_000);\n    const midCheck = await getEngineState(page);\n    const stoveMid = midCheck.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'stove');\n    expect(\n      stoveMid?.state,\n      'Stove returned to idle before 30s \u2014 auto_next timer too short'\n    ).not.toBe('idle');\n    console.log(`Stove state at 20s: ${stoveMid?.state}`);\n\n    // Wait for auto-reset (up to 45s total)\n    const returned = await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'stove')?.state === 'idle',\n      45_000,\n      1000\n    );\n    const stoveAfter = returned.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'stove');\n    expect(stoveAfter.state, 'Stove should return to idle after activity duration').toBe('idle');\n    console.log('Stove returned to idle as expected.');\n  });\n\n  test('interacting character stays near object tile during activity', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * When a character is interacting with an object, their tile position should\n     * remain close to the object's tile (within 2 tiles) for the duration of the interaction.\n     * We monitor the bonsai interaction since it completes quickly (12s).\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Trigger the bonsai interaction\n    await page.evaluate(() => {\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bonsai', 'use');\n    });\n\n    // Poll until a character is in an interact-like action (not walking)\n    // Sample character positions every 2s while bonsai is active\n    const positionSamples = [];\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(1500);\n      const state = await getEngineState(page);\n      const bonsai = state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');\n      if (bonsai?.state === 'idle') break; // auto-reset already happened\n      positionSamples.push(state.characters.map(c => ({ label: c.label, tx: c.tx, ty: c.ty, action: c.action })));\n    }\n\n    console.log('Character position samples during bonsai interaction:', JSON.stringify(positionSamples, null, 2));\n\n    // Verify all sampled positions are within walkable bounds\n    for (const sample of positionSamples) {\n      for (const c of sample) {\n        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of walkable bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of walkable bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(17);\n        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of walkable bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);\n        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of walkable bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(9);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n\n  test('bed object (long-duration) remains active for at least 10 real seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * The bed has a longer activity duration than the bonsai.\n     * Trigger it and confirm it does not prematurely return to idle.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    await page.evaluate(() => {\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bed', 'use');\n    });\n\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    const activated = await getEngineState(page);\n    const bed = activated.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bed');\n    expect(bed?.state, 'Bed should enter active state after interact').not.toBe('idle');\n    console.log(`Bed state after trigger: ${bed?.state}`);\n\n    // After 10s it should still be active (bed duration should be > 10s)\n    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);\n    const midCheck = await getEngineState(page);\n    const bedMid = midCheck.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bed');\n    expect(\n      bedMid?.state,\n      'Bed returned to idle within 10s \u2014 activity duration is shorter than expected'\n    ).not.toBe('idle');\n    console.log(`Bed state at 10s: ${bedMid?.state}`);\n  });\n\n  test('multiple objects can be active simultaneously', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * Verifies concurrent object usage \u2014 two objects triggered at the same time\n     * should both transition to active states independently.\n     */\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Trigger two objects simultaneously\n    await page.evaluate(() => {\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bonsai', 'use');\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('tv', 'use');\n    });\n\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    const state = await getEngineState(page);\n    const bonsai = state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');\n    const tv = state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'tv');\n\n    expect(bonsai?.state, 'Bonsai should be active').not.toBe('idle');\n    expect(tv?.state, 'TV should be active').not.toBe('idle');\n    console.log(`Bonsai: ${bonsai?.state}, TV: ${tv?.state}`);\n  });\n});\n"
}

    `${name} sent ${count} request(s) \u2014 expected \u22652 across 65s (two 30s cycles)`\n      ).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);\n    }\n\n    console.log('LLM request counts per character:', Object.fromEntries(byChar));\n  });\n\n  test('stove object stays in active state for ~30 seconds before returning to idle', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * The stove's activity duration is 30 real seconds.\n     * Trigger it via the engine and verify it doesn't auto-reset prematurely.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Manually trigger the stove via the engine\n    await page.evaluate(() => {\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('stove', 'use');\n    });\n\n    // Verify stove transitioned to an active state (not idle)\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    const activated = await getEngineState(page);\n    const stove = activated.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'stove');\n    expect(stove?.state, 'Stove should enter active state after interact').not.toBe('idle');\n    console.log(`Stove state after trigger: ${stove?.state}`);\n\n    // Check at 20s \u2014 stove must still be active (not yet auto-reset)\n    await page.waitForTimeout(20_000);\n    const midCheck = await getEngineState(page);\n    const stoveMid = midCheck.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'stove');\n    expect(\n      stoveMid?.state,\n      'Stove returned to idle before 30s \u2014 auto_next timer too short'\n    ).not.toBe('idle');\n    console.log(`Stove state at 20s: ${stoveMid?.state}`);\n\n    // Wait for auto-reset (up to 45s total)\n    const returned = await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'stove')?.state === 'idle',\n      45_000,\n      1000\n    );\n    const stoveAfter = returned.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'stove');\n    expect(stoveAfter.state, 'Stove should return to idle after activity duration').toBe('idle');\n    console.log('Stove returned to idle as expected.');\n  });\n\n  test('interacting character stays near object tile during activity', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * When a character is interacting with an object, their tile position should\n     * remain close to the object's tile (within 2 tiles) for the duration of the interaction.\n     * We monitor the bonsai interaction since it completes quickly (12s).\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Trigger the bonsai interaction\n    await page.evaluate(() => {\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bonsai', 'use');\n    });\n\n    // Poll until a character is in an interact-like action (not walking)\n    // Sample character positions every 2s while bonsai is active\n    const positionSamples = [];\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(1500);\n      const state = await getEngineState(page);\n      const bonsai = state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');\n      if (bonsai?.state === 'idle') break; // auto-reset already happened\n      positionSamples.push(state.characters.map(c => ({ label: c.label, tx: c.tx, ty: c.ty, action: c.action })));\n    }\n\n    console.log('Character position samples during bonsai interaction:', JSON.stringify(positionSamples, null, 2));\n\n    // Verify all sampled positions are within walkable bounds\n    for (const sample of positionSamples) {\n      for (const c of sample) {\n        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of walkable bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of walkable bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(17);\n        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of walkable bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);\n        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of walkable bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(9);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n\n  test('bed object (long-duration) remains active for at least 10 real seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * The bed has a longer activity duration than the bonsai.\n     * Trigger it and confirm it does not prematurely return to idle.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    await page.evaluate(() => {\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bed', 'use');\n    });\n\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    const activated = await getEngineState(page);\n    const bed = activated.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bed');\n    expect(bed?.state, 'Bed should enter active state after interact').not.toBe('idle');\n    console.log(`Bed state after trigger: ${bed?.state}`);\n\n    // After 10s it should still be active (bed duration should be > 10s)\n    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);\n    const midCheck = await getEngineState(page);\n    const bedMid = midCheck.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bed');\n    expect(\n      bedMid?.state,\n      'Bed returned to idle within 10s \u2014 activity duration is shorter than expected'\n    ).not.toBe('idle');\n    console.log(`Bed state at 10s: ${bedMid?.state}`);\n  });\n\n  test('multiple objects can be active simultaneously', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * Verifies concurrent object usage \u2014 two objects triggered at the same time\n     * should both transition to active states independently.\n     */\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n\n    // Trigger two objects simultaneously\n    await page.evaluate(() => {\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bonsai', 'use');\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('tv', 'use');\n    });\n\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    const state = await getEngineState(page);\n    const bonsai = state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');\n    const tv = state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'tv');\n\n    expect(bonsai?.state, 'Bonsai should be active').not.toBe('idle');\n    expect(tv?.state, 'TV should be active').not.toBe('idle');\n    console.log(`Bonsai: ${bonsai?.state}, TV: ${tv?.state}`);\n  });\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/debug-panel-extended.spec.js",
  "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-209\n * Verifies that the debug page shows character states, object states,\n * and a decision log in real-time.\n *\n * Complements the smoke test (app-loads.spec.js) which only checks that\n * the debug panel opens and the OBJECT STATES section is visible.\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n\ntest.describe('Regression: Debug panel \u2014 character states and decision log', () => {\n  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n    // Open the debug panel\n    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();\n  });\n\n  test('debug panel shows each character name in real-time', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * The debug panel must display all 3 character names so the developer\n     * can observe their live state.\n     */\n    await expect(page.getByText('Maple', { exact: false })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 });\n    await expect(page.getByText('Sol', { exact: false })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 });\n    await expect(page.getByText('Nyx', { exact: false })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 });\n  });\n\n  test('debug panel shows character action states in real-time', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * The character section should reflect the live action (idle, walking, interacting\u2026).\n     * We poll until at least one character is walking, then verify the panel updates.\n     */\n    const walkState = await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n      15_000\n    );\n\n    const walkerName = walkState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking').label;\n\n    // The debug panel should display the current action for that character\n    // Action text may be rendered directly or as a data row\n    const actionText = page.getByText(/walking|idle|interacting/i);\n    await expect(actionText.first()).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });\n\n    console.log(`Debug panel validated for ${walkerName} in walking state`);\n  });\n\n  test('debug panel character section shows mood and energy values', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * Mood and energy for each character should be visible in the debug panel\n     * so developers can monitor stat changes over time.\n     */\n    const state = await getEngineState(page);\n\n    // All characters start at mood=100, energy=100 \u2014 these values should appear in panel\n    // Look for numeric values that match the engine state\n    const panelText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    for (const c of state.characters) {\n      // The debug panel should at minimum show mood or energy for each character\n      const hasCharInfo = panelText.includes(c.label) ||\n                          panelText.includes(c.name);\n      expect(hasCharInfo, `Debug panel should show character ${c.label}`).toBe(true);\n    }\n\n    console.log('Character labels found in debug panel text');\n  });\n\n  test('debug panel decision log updates after AI fires', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * After the first AI decision cycle, the debug panel should show a log or history\n     * of recent decisions. This may be labelled \"DECISION LOG\", \"EVENT LOG\", \"AI LOG\",\n     * or similar.\n     */\n    // Wait for at least one AI decision to fire\n    await page.waitForTimeout(8_000);\n\n    const panelText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Check for any decision log heading (case-insensitive)\n    const hasDecisionLog =\n      /decision\\s*log|event\\s*log|ai\\s*log|decision\\s*history|recent\\s*decision/i.test(panelText) ||\n      // Or check if individual decision actions appear in the panel\n      /move|interact|idle|talk/i.test(panelText);\n\n    expect(hasDecisionLog, 'Debug panel should show a decision log or decision action labels').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('Decision-related text found in debug panel');\n  });\n\n  test('debug panel OBJECT STATES updates when objects change state', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * This verifies the OBJECT STATES section in the debug panel reflects live changes.\n     * Extended from smoke test \u2014 here we also check the update happens.\n     */\n    const initial = await getEngineState(page);\n    const initialMap = Object.fromEntries(initial.objectStates.map(o => [o.id, o.state]));\n\n    // Trigger a state change via engine directly\n    await page.evaluate(() => {\n      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('kotatsu', 'use');\n    });\n\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    const after = await getEngineState(page);\n    const kotatsu = after.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'kotatsu');\n\n    expect(kotatsu?.state, 'Kotatsu should have changed state').not.toBe('idle');\n\n    // The new state should appear in the debug panel\n    const newState = kotatsu?.state;\n    const stateVisible = page.getByText(newState, { exact: false });\n    await expect(stateVisible).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 });\n\n    console.log(`Debug panel shows kotatsu state: ${newState}`);\n  });\n\n  test('debug panel reflects all 10 objects with their IDs or labels', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * Every interactive object should be listed in the debug panel.\n     */\n    const state = await getEngineState(page);\n    const panelText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    const expectedObjects = ['Stove', 'Bed', 'Kotatsu', 'Bonsai', 'Bookshelf', 'TV', 'Desk', 'Easel', 'Tea Set', 'Fridge'];\n    const foundObjects = expectedObjects.filter(name => panelText.includes(name));\n\n    console.log(`Objects visible in debug panel: ${foundObjects.join(', ')}`);\n    expect(\n      foundObjects.length,\n      `Expected all 10 objects in debug panel, found ${foundObjects.length}: ${foundObjects.join(', ')}`\n    ).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(8); // allow for some label variation\n  });\n});\n"
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #62
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/pathfinding-walls.spec.js",
  "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-209\n * Verifies that pathfinding respects the walkable grid \u2014 characters must not\n * pass through walls or non-walkable tiles.\n *\n * The walkable region is cols 1\u201317, rows 2\u20139. Within that region there are\n * also internal walls (e.g. furniture footprints that block movement).\n * We verify via:\n *  1. Continuous position sampling \u2014 all positions must stay within bounds\n *  2. A* pathfinding does not teleport (movement is step-by-step, not jump)\n *  3. Characters never occupy the same tile as a non-walkable object footprint\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n\ntest.describe('Regression: Pathfinding respects walkable grid', () => {\n  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n  });\n\n  test('character positions never exceed walkable grid bounds during movement', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * Sample all character positions every second for 20s while characters move.\n     * No position should exit the 1\u201317 \u00d7 2\u20139 walkable region.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(45_000);\n\n    // Wait for movement to begin\n    await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n      15_000\n    );\n\n    const violations = [];\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 15; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(1000);\n      const state = await getEngineState(page);\n      for (const c of state.characters) {\n        if (c.tx < 1 || c.tx > 17 || c.ty < 2 || c.ty > 9) {\n          violations.push({ label: c.label, tx: c.tx, ty: c.ty, action: c.action, tick: i });\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    expect(\n      violations,\n      `Characters exited walkable bounds: ${JSON.stringify(violations)}`\n    ).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n\n  test('walking character moves in steps (no teleportation)', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * A* pathfinding should produce smooth step-by-step tile movement.\n     * Between consecutive 500ms samples, a character should move at most 2 tiles\n     * in each direction \u2014 teleportation (>3 tile jumps) would indicate broken pathfinding.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n      15_000\n    );\n\n    let prevState = await getEngineState(page);\n    const teleports = [];\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n      const currState = await getEngineState(page);\n\n      for (const curr of currState.characters) {\n        if (curr.action !== 'walking') continue;\n        const prev = prevState.characters.find(c => c.name === curr.name);\n        if (!prev) continue;\n\n        const dx = Math.abs(curr.tx - prev.tx);\n        const dy = Math.abs(curr.ty - prev.ty);\n\n        // Max movement per 500ms: 2 tiles (generous upper bound)\n        if (dx > 3 || dy > 3) {\n          teleports.push({ label: curr.label, from: { tx: prev.tx, ty: prev.ty }, to: { tx: curr.tx, ty: curr.ty }, dx, dy });\n        }\n      }\n\n      prevState = currState;\n    }\n\n    expect(\n      teleports,\n      `Teleportation detected: ${JSON.stringify(teleports)}`\n    ).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n\n  test('A* path does not cross known wall tiles (row 1 \u2014 top boundary)', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * Row 1 (ty=1) is outside the walkable grid (grid starts at row 2).\n     * No character should ever occupy ty=1.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    const wallViolations = [];\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 20; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n      const state = await getEngineState(page);\n      for (const c of state.characters) {\n        if (c.ty <= 1) {\n          wallViolations.push({ label: c.label, ty: c.ty, tick: i });\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    expect(\n      wallViolations,\n      `Characters walked into wall row (ty\u22641): ${JSON.stringify(wallViolations)}`\n    ).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n\n  test('walkable grid is exposed via engine and matches expected dimensions', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * The engine should expose its walkable grid. We verify the grid exists\n     * and has the expected dimensions (19 cols \u00d7 10 rows, 0-indexed).\n     */\n    const gridInfo = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const eng = window._gameEngine;\n      // Try common property names for the walkable grid\n      const grid = eng.walkableGrid ?? eng.grid ?? eng.pathfinder?.grid ?? eng.navGrid;\n      if (!grid) return null;\n\n      const rows = Array.isArray(grid) ? grid.length : (grid.height ?? grid.rows ?? null);\n      const cols = Array.isArray(grid) && Array.isArray(grid[0]) ? grid[0].length : (grid.width ?? grid.cols ?? null);\n\n      return { hasGrid: true, rows, cols, type: typeof grid };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Walkable grid info:', JSON.stringify(gridInfo));\n\n    if (gridInfo === null) {\n      console.log('Walkable grid not directly exposed on engine \u2014 skipping dimension check');\n      // This is acceptable \u2014 bounds are verified by position sampling tests above\n      return;\n    }\n\n    expect(gridInfo.hasGrid).toBe(true);\n    // Grid rows should cover at minimum rows 0\u20139 (10 rows)\n    if (gridInfo.rows !== null) {\n      expect(gridInfo.rows, 'Grid should have at least 10 rows').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(10);\n    }\n  });\n\n  test('character pathfinding destination is reachable \u2014 engine confirms path exists', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * When a character receives a move decision with a targetTile, the engine\n     * should successfully compute an A* path (no path-not-found errors).\n     * We intercept and validate decisions vs. engine path computation.\n     */\n    const moveDecisions = [];\n\n    page.on('response', async res => {\n      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {\n        try {\n          const json = await res.json();\n          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n          if (text) {\n            const d = JSON.parse(text);\n            if (d.action === 'move' && d.targetTile) moveDecisions.push(d);\n          }\n        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n      }\n    });\n\n    const pathErrors = [];\n    page.on('console', msg => {\n      const text = msg.text();\n      if (/no\\s*path|path\\s*not\\s*found|pathfinding\\s*fail/i.test(text)) {\n        pathErrors.push(text);\n      }\n    });\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);\n\n    console.log(`Move decisions received: ${moveDecisions.length}`);\n    console.log(`Path errors detected: ${pathErrors.length}`);\n    if (pathErrors.length > 0) console.log('Path errors:', pathErrors);\n\n    // No pathfinding errors should occur for valid AI-issued move targets\n    expect(\n      pathErrors,\n      `A* pathfinding failed for some targets: ${pathErrors.join(' | ')}`\n    ).toHaveLength(0);\n\n    if (moveDecisions.length > 0) {\n      // All move targets should be within the walkable region\n      for (const d of moveDecisions) {\n        expect(d.targetTile.tx, `Move target tx=${d.targetTile.tx} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n        expect(d.targetTile.tx, `Move target tx=${d.targetTile.tx} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(17);\n        expect(d.targetTile.ty, `Move target ty=${d.targetTile.ty} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);\n        expect(d.targetTile.ty, `Move target ty=${d.targetTile.ty} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(9);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n});\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_015AEBnJKMan4zLcCRBcLLRt",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/pathfinding-walls.spec.js",
      "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-209\n * Verifies that pathfinding respects the walkable grid \u2014 characters must not\n * pass through walls or non-walkable tiles.\n *\n * The walkable region is cols 1\u201317, rows 2\u20139. Within that region there are\n * also internal walls (e.g. furniture footprints that block movement).\n * We verify via:\n *  1. Continuous position sampling \u2014 all positions must stay within bounds\n *  2. A* pathfinding does not teleport (movement is step-by-step, not jump)\n *  3. Characters never occupy the same tile as a non-walkable object footprint\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n\ntest.describe('Regression: Pathfinding respects walkable grid', () => {\n  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n  });\n\n  test('character positions never exceed walkable grid bounds during movement', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * Sample all character positions every second for 20s while characters move.\n     * No position should exit the 1\u201317 \u00d7 2\u20139 walkable region.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(45_000);\n\n    // Wait for movement to begin\n    await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n      15_000\n    );\n\n    const violations = [];\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 15; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(1000);\n      const state = await getEngineState(page);\n      for (const c of state.characters) {\n        if (c.tx < 1 || c.tx > 17 || c.ty < 2 || c.ty > 9) {\n          violations.push({ label: c.label, tx: c.tx, ty: c.ty, action: c.action, tick: i });\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    expect(\n      violations,\n      `Characters exited walkable bounds: ${JSON.stringify(violations)}`\n    ).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n\n  test('walking character moves in steps (no teleportation)', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * A* pathfinding should produce smooth step-by-step tile movement.\n     * Between consecutive 500ms samples, a character should move at most 2 tiles\n     * in each direction \u2014 teleportation (>3 tile jumps) would indicate broken pathfinding.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n      15_000\n    );\n\n    let prevState = await getEngineState(page);\n    const teleports = [];\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n      const currState = await getEngineState(page);\n\n      for (const curr of currState.characters) {\n        if (curr.action !== 'walking') continue;\n        const prev = prevState.characters.find(c => c.name === curr.name);\n        if (!prev) continue;\n\n        const dx = Math.abs(curr.tx - prev.tx);\n        const dy = Math.abs(curr.ty - prev.ty);\n\n        // Max movement per 500ms: 2 tiles (generous upper bound)\n        if (dx > 3 || dy > 3) {\n          teleports.push({ label: curr.label, from: { tx: prev.tx, ty: prev.ty }, to: { tx: curr.tx, ty: curr.ty }, dx, dy });\n        }\n      }\n\n      prevState = currState;\n    }\n\n    expect(\n      teleports,\n      `Teleportation detected: ${JSON.stringify(teleports)}`\n    ).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n\n  test('A* path does not cross known wall tiles (row 1 \u2014 top boundary)', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * Row 1 (ty=1) is outside the walkable grid (grid starts at row 2).\n     * No character should ever occupy ty=1.\n     */\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    const wallViolations = [];\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 20; i++) {\n      await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n      const state = await getEngineState(page);\n      for (const c of state.characters) {\n        if (c.ty <= 1) {\n          wallViolations.push({ label: c.label, ty: c.ty, tick: i });\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    expect(\n      wallViolations,\n      `Characters walked into wall row (ty\u22641): ${JSON.stringify(wallViolations)}`\n    ).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n\n  test('walkable grid is exposed via engine and matches expected dimensions', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * The engine should expose its walkable grid. We verify the grid exists\n     * and has the expected dimensions (19 cols \u00d7 10 rows, 0-indexed).\n     */\n    const gridInfo = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const eng = window._gameEngine;\n      // Try common property names for the walkable grid\n      const grid = eng.walkableGrid ?? eng.grid ?? eng.pathfinder?.grid ?? eng.navGrid;\n      if (!grid) return null;\n\n      const rows = Array.isArray(grid) ? grid.length : (grid.height ?? grid.rows ?? null);\n      const cols = Array.isArray(grid) && Array.isArray(grid[0]) ? grid[0].length : (grid.width ?? grid.cols ?? null);\n\n      return { hasGrid: true, rows, cols, type: typeof grid };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Walkable grid info:', JSON.stringify(gridInfo));\n\n    if (gridInfo === null) {\n      console.log('Walkable grid not directly exposed on engine \u2014 skipping dimension check');\n      // This is acceptable \u2014 bounds are verified by position sampling tests above\n      return;\n    }\n\n    expect(gridInfo.hasGrid).toBe(true);\n    // Grid rows should cover at minimum rows 0\u20139 (10 rows)\n    if (gridInfo.rows !== null) {\n      expect(gridInfo.rows, 'Grid should have at least 10 rows').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(10);\n    }\n  });\n\n  test('character pathfinding destination is reachable \u2014 engine confirms path exists', async ({ page }) => {\n    /**\n     * When a character receives a move decision with a targetTile, the engine\n     * should successfully compute an A* path (no path-not-found errors).\n     * We intercept and validate decisions vs. engine path computation.\n     */\n    const moveDecisions = [];\n\n    page.on('response', async res => {\n      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {\n        try {\n          const json = await res.json();\n          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n          if (text) {\n            const d = JSON.parse(text);\n            if (d.action === 'move' && d.targetTile) moveDecisions.push(d);\n          }\n        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n      }\n    });\n\n    const pathErrors = [];\n    page.on('console', msg => {\n      const text = msg.text();\n      if (/no\\s*path|path\\s*not\\s*found|pathfinding\\s*fail/i.test(text)) {\n        pathErrors.push(text);\n      }\n    });\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);\n\n    console.log(`Move decisions received: ${moveDecisions.length}`);\n    console.log(`Path errors detected: ${pathErrors.length}`);\n    if (pathErrors.length > 0) console.log('Path errors:', pathErrors);\n\n    // No pathfinding errors should occur for valid AI-issued move targets\n    expect(\n      pathErrors,\n      `A* pathfinding failed for some targets: ${pathErrors.join(' | ')}`\n    ).toHaveLength(0);\n\n    if (moveDecisions.length > 0) {\n      // All move targets should be within the walkable region\n      for (const d of moveDecisions) {\n        expect(d.targetTile.tx, `Move target tx=${d.targetTile.tx} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n        expect(d.targetTile.tx, `Move target tx=${d.targetTile.tx} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(17);\n        expect(d.targetTile.ty, `Move target ty=${d.targetTile.ty} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);\n        expect(d.targetTile.ty, `Move target ty=${d.targetTile.ty} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(9);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #68
Two debug panel tests are failing: 1. `debug panel shows each character name in real-time` - "Maple" is not visible in the debug panel 2. `debug panel character section shows mood and energy values` - Character names are not found in the debug panel text This is revealing! The debug panel does NOT show character names (Maple, Sol, Nyx) in its text. This is a BUG / GAP - the debug panel shows object states but apparently doesn't show character states in a way that includes their names. Let me investigate what the debug panel actually contains. I need to probe the actual DOM content of the debug panel. Let me use the browser to look at what text is in the panel after it's opened. Actually, looking at the test output from the smoke test, we know the debug panel shows "OBJECT STATES" heading and labels like "Stove" for objects. But the character tests are checking for both the label ("Maple") and the internal name ("Amelia"), and neither appears in the panel text. So the debug panel currently displays object states but is missing character state information entirely — that's the gap I need to fix. The acceptance criteria for FRG-209 requires showing character states, object states, and decision logs all in real-time, so I should rewrite these tests to first investigate what's actually rendered in the DOM versus what might be stuck in the Phaser canvas, then document the missing character state display as a bug that needs fixing. I'm realizing the core issue: these tests should document what's actually missing from the debug panel rather than fail cryptically. The character states section simply isn't being rendered, so I need to rewrite the tests to clearly probe for this missing feature and report it as a legitimate gap in the implementation, not a test failure.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #72
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/debug-panel-probe.js << 'EOF'\nconst { chromium } = require('playwright');\nrequire('dotenv').config();\n\n(async () => {\n  const browser = await chromium.launch({ args: ['--enable-unsafe-swiftshader', '--disable-gpu'] });\n  const page = await browser.newPage();\n  await page.goto(process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app');\n  \n  // Wait for engine\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => window._gameEngine && window._gameEngine.characters && window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,\n    { timeout: 20000 }\n  );\n  \n  // Open debug panel\n  const debugBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i });\n  await debugBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n  \n  // Get full page text\n  const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n  console.log('=== DEBUG PANEL TEXT ===');\n  console.log(bodyText.substring(0, 3000));\n  \n  // Get all visible text nodes in the debug panel\n  const panelHTML = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const panel = document.querySelector('[class*=\"debug\"]') ||\n                  document.querySelector('[id*=\"debug\"]') ||\n                  document.querySelector('[data-testid*=\"debug\"]');\n    return panel ? panel.innerHTML.substring(0, 2000) : 'Panel not found via selector';\n  });\n  console.log('=== PANEL HTML ===');\n  console.log(panelHTML.substring(0, 2000));\n  \n  await browser.close();\n})();\nEOF\nnode /tmp/debug-panel-probe.js 2>&1",
  "timeout": 60000
}

      "command": "cat > /tmp/debug-panel-probe.js << 'EOF'\nconst { chromium } = require('playwright');\nrequire('dotenv').config();\n\n(async () => {\n  const browser = await chromium.launch({ args: ['--enable-unsafe-swiftshader', '--disable-gpu'] });\n  const page = await browser.newPage();\n  await page.goto(process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app');\n  \n  // Wait for engine\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => window._gameEngine && window._gameEngine.characters && window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,\n    { timeout: 20000 }\n  );\n  \n  // Open debug panel\n  const debugBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i });\n  await debugBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n  \n  // Get full page text\n  const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n  console.log('=== DEBUG PANEL TEXT ===');\n  console.log(bodyText.substring(0, 3000));\n  \n  // Get all visible text nodes in the debug panel\n  const panelHTML = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const panel = document.querySelector('[class*=\"debug\"]') ||\n                  document.querySelector('[id*=\"debug\"]') ||\n                  document.querySelector('[data-testid*=\"debug\"]');\n    return panel ? panel.innerHTML.substring(0, 2000) : 'Panel not found via selector';\n  });\n  console.log('=== PANEL HTML ===');\n  console.log(panelHTML.substring(0, 2000));\n  \n  await browser.close();\n})();\nEOF\nnode /tmp/debug-panel-probe.js 2>&1",
      "timeout": 60000
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/probe-spec.js << 'EOF'\nconst { test } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine } = require('./tests/helpers');\n\ntest('probe debug panel content', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await waitForEngine(page);\n  const debugBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i });\n  await debugBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n  \n  const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n  console.log('=== BODY TEXT ===\\n' + bodyText.substring(0, 4000));\n});\nEOF\nnpx playwright test /tmp/probe-spec.js --reporter=list 2>&1",
  "timeout": 60000
}

Now I can see what the debug panel actually shows: - "Debug ✕" (title/close button) - "OBJECT STATES" section - A sim time (8:01 AM) - All 10 objects with state and "use" button - "↗ Full dashboard" link at the bottom It does NOT show: - Character names (Maple, Sol, Nyx) - Character states (action, mood, energy) - Decision log The panel is currently limited to OBJECT STATES. There's also a "Full dashboard" link! That might be where character states and decision log are shown. Let me probe the full dashboard link. So this confirms a real gap—the debug panel only displays object states when FRG-209 actually requires character states, object states, and the decision log all in real-time. The two failing tests are correctly identifying missing features, so I need to update them to reflect what's actually implemented versus what's required. Now I'm checking whether the "Full dashboard" link leads to a separate page that might contain the missing information.

{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/probe-debug.spec.js",
  "content": "'use strict';\nconst { test } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine } = require('./helpers');\n\ntest('probe full dashboard content', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await waitForEngine(page);\n  const debugBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i });\n  await debugBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(1000);\n\n  // Click \"Full dashboard\"\n  const fullDashLink = page.getByText(/full dashboard/i);\n  const href = await fullDashLink.getAttribute('href').catch(() => null);\n  console.log('Full dashboard href:', href);\n\n  await fullDashLink.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(3000);\n\n  const url = page.url();\n  console.log('Current URL after clicking:', url);\n\n  const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n  console.log('=== FULL DASHBOARD TEXT ===\\n' + bodyText.substring(0, 6000));\n});\n"
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #91
Excellent! Now I can see the full debug page at `/debug.html`! It shows: - **OBJECTS (10)** section with: object name, state, tile position, timer remaining, use button - **CHARACTERS** section with: name, action, position, facing direction, target, mood, energy, hold timer, AI next timer - **EVENT LOG** section with: timestamp, entity, event description, and trigger (AI or auto) This is the complete debug page! The sidebar debug panel on `/` is just a mini-view with only object states. The FULL debug dashboard at `/debug.html` shows everything:- Character states (action, position, facing, target, mood, energy) - Object states (name, state, tile, timer) - Event log (with AI decisions and auto transitions) So the issue description says "Test against the debug page (not main page — that is M2)". The debug page IS `/debug.html`, not `/`! This is a critical realization: all the existing tests are navigating to `/` (the main sim page with a mini debug sidebar), not to `/debug.html` (the actual full debug dashboard). The issue explicitly says to test against the debug page, which is `debug.html`. But the existing FRG-184 tests were passing and they use `window._gameEngine`, which works at `/`. I need to figure out whether `debug.html` also exposes `window._gameEngine` or if I need a different approach. Looking at the debug.html content, it displays real-time game state like "8:04 AM" and object timers, so it's definitely connected to the game engine somehow. The question is whether it's reading from a shared `window._gameEngine` object, using postMessage communication with a parent window, or running its own standalone instance of the engine. Let me reconsider how the debug page actually works. The test navigates to `/debug.html` and sees live character data (Maple interacting, Sol idle, Nyx walking), which means the debug page has its own game engine instance running. So `waitForEngine` should work there too. This means I need to update the FRG-209 tests to navigate to `/debug.html` instead of `/`, write tests for the CHARACTER STATES and EVENT LOG sections on that page, and keep the existing FRG-184 tests on `/` for the main sim page. But I should also consider whether the existing tests need updating too. Actually, the FRG-184 tests are fine as-is since they test the main page's Phaser canvas functionality. For FRG-209, I'll navigate to `/debug.html` for debug-specific features, test both the CHARACTER STATES and EVENT LOG sections there, and handle the sidebar debug panel on `/` separately. Now I need to fix the debug-panel-extended.spec.js file to point to `/debug.html` for character-related tests and add new tests for the EVENT LOG and CHARACTER STATES sections. The two failing tests are looking at the sidebar panel on `/` which doesn't display character names—those are only on `/debug.html`. I'll update those tests to navigate to the debug page instead. I also need to add comprehensive tests for the EVENT LOG section to verify it shows AI decisions, auto transitions, and real-time character actions with their associated data like mood, energy, and AI timers.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #93
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/debug-panel-extended.spec.js",
  "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-209\n * Validates the full debug dashboard at /debug.html.\n *\n * The sidebar at / shows only a mini OBJECT STATES panel.\n * The full dashboard at /debug.html shows:\n *   - OBJECTS section (name, state, tile, timer, use button)\n *   - CHARACTERS section (name, action, position, target, mood, energy, AI next timer)\n *   - EVENT LOG (AI decisions and auto transitions with timestamps)\n *\n * FRG-209 acceptance: \"Debug page shows: character states, object states,\n * decision log, all in real-time\"\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n\nconst DEBUG_URL = '/debug.html';\n\n/** Wait for the debug.html page to be hydrated with live data */\nasync function waitForDebugPage(page, timeout = 25_000) {\n  await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n  // Wait until the CHARACTERS section is populated with at least one character name\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const text = document.body.innerText;\n      return text.includes('CHARACTERS') && (\n        text.includes('Maple') || text.includes('Sol') || text.includes('Nyx')\n      );\n    },\n    { timeout }\n  );\n}\n\ntest.describe('Regression: Debug dashboard (/debug.html) \u2014 character states and event log', () => {\n  test('debug.html page loads and shows sim clock', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/AI Aquarium/i);\n    // Clock matches HH:MM AM/PM format\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => /\\d+:\\d{2}\\s*(AM|PM)/.test(document.body.innerText),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n    expect(bodyText).toMatch(/\\d+:\\d{2}\\s*(AM|PM)/);\n    console.log('Debug dashboard loaded with sim clock');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html OBJECTS section shows all 10 objects with tile positions', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => document.body.innerText.includes('OBJECTS'),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // OBJECTS heading\n    expect(bodyText).toMatch(/OBJECTS\\s*\\(10\\)/);\n\n    // All 10 object names\n    const expected = ['Stove', 'Fridge', 'Tea Set', 'Kotatsu', 'TV', 'Bookshelf', 'Desk', 'Bed', 'Easel', 'Bonsai'];\n    for (const obj of expected) {\n      expect(bodyText, `\"${obj}\" should appear in debug dashboard OBJECTS section`).toContain(obj);\n    }\n\n    // Tile positions are shown\n    expect(bodyText).toMatch(/tile \\(\\d+, \\d+\\)/);\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section shows all 3 character names in real-time', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(bodyText, 'CHARACTERS section heading missing').toContain('CHARACTERS');\n    expect(bodyText, 'Maple not found in CHARACTERS section').toContain('Maple');\n    expect(bodyText, 'Sol not found in CHARACTERS section').toContain('Sol');\n    expect(bodyText, 'Nyx not found in CHARACTERS section').toContain('Nyx');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section shows action, mood, and energy for each character', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Mood and energy values should appear (characters start at 100)\n    expect(bodyText, 'Mood labels missing from CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/Mood/);\n    expect(bodyText, 'Energy labels missing from CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/Energy/);\n\n    // Action labels\n    expect(bodyText, 'Action labels missing from CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/Action/);\n\n    // Mood/energy values (100 at start)\n    expect(bodyText, 'Expected \"100\" for mood/energy in CHARACTERS').toContain('100');\n\n    console.log('CHARACTERS section contains action, mood, energy \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section shows position and AI next timer', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Position shown as (x, y)\n    expect(bodyText, 'Character position not shown').toMatch(/\\(\\d+, \\d+\\)/);\n\n    // AI next timer (seconds remaining until next decision)\n    expect(bodyText, 'AI next timer not shown in CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/AI next\\s*\\d+s/);\n\n    console.log('CHARACTERS section shows position and AI next timer \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html EVENT LOG shows AI decisions after first cycle', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    // Wait for EVENT LOG section to appear and be populated\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => {\n        const text = document.body.innerText;\n        return text.includes('EVENT LOG') && /AI/.test(text);\n      },\n      { timeout: 20_000 }\n    );\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(bodyText, 'EVENT LOG heading missing').toContain('EVENT LOG');\n    // AI-sourced entries should appear\n    expect(bodyText, 'No AI entries in EVENT LOG').toMatch(/AI/);\n    // Entries include character names\n    const hasCharInLog = /Maple|Sol|Nyx/.test(bodyText);\n    expect(hasCharInLog, 'No character names found in EVENT LOG').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('EVENT LOG contains AI decisions \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html EVENT LOG shows auto state transitions', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(8_000); // allow some auto-transitions\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Auto transitions (e.g. \"idle \u2192 brewing\" or \"auto\" label)\n    // These may appear as \"auto\" source or as idle\u2192active transitions\n    const hasAutoOrTransition = /auto|idle\\s*\u2192|\u2192\\s*idle/.test(bodyText);\n    if (!hasAutoOrTransition) {\n      console.log('No auto transitions observed yet \u2014 may require longer observation window');\n    }\n\n    // EVENT LOG section must exist\n    expect(bodyText, 'EVENT LOG section missing from debug dashboard').toContain('EVENT LOG');\n\n    console.log('EVENT LOG verified \u2014 section exists and is populating');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html object state changes are reflected in real-time in OBJECTS section', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => document.body.innerText.includes('OBJECTS'),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    // Click \"use\" on the bonsai to trigger a state change\n    const useButtons = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'use' });\n    await useButtons.first().click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Some object should now show a non-idle state\n    const hasActiveState = /tending|brewing|cooking|cozy|watching|reading|sleeping|sketching|working|researching/i.test(bodyText);\n    expect(hasActiveState, 'No active object state visible after clicking \"use\" in debug dashboard').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('Debug dashboard reflects real-time object state changes \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section updates when character action changes', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    // Record character actions at t=0\n    const before = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Wait for movement to potentially change some character state\n    await page.waitForTimeout(5_000);\n    const after = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // The page content should change (indicating real-time updates)\n    // At minimum the AI next timer countdown should have decremented\n    const changed = before !== after;\n    expect(changed, 'Debug dashboard content did not update over 5s \u2014 page may be static').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('Debug dashboard updates in real-time \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html Pause/Resume controls exist and are functional', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => /Pause|Resume/.test(document.body.innerText),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const pauseBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /Pause|Resume/i });\n    await expect(pauseBtn).toBeVisible();\n\n    // Click pause \u2014 sim clock should stop advancing\n    await pauseBtn.click();\n    const t1 = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText.match(/\\d+:\\d{2}\\s*(AM|PM)/)?.[0]);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(3_000);\n    const t2 = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText.match(/\\d+:\\d{2}\\s*(AM|PM)/)?.[0]);\n\n    console.log(`Sim time before pause-check: ${t1}, after 3s: ${t2}`);\n    // If paused, t1 and t2 should be equal OR the page should show \"Resume\" button\n    const resumeBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /Resume/i });\n    const isPaused = await resumeBtn.isVisible().catch(() => false) ||\n                     (await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Pause/i }).isVisible().catch(() => false));\n    expect(isPaused, 'Pause button or Resume button should be visible').toBe(true);\n  });\n});\n"
}

      "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-209\n * Validates the full debug dashboard at /debug.html.\n *\n * The sidebar at / shows only a mini OBJECT STATES panel.\n * The full dashboard at /debug.html shows:\n *   - OBJECTS section (name, state, tile, timer, use button)\n *   - CHARACTERS section (name, action, position, target, mood, energy, AI next timer)\n *   - EVENT LOG (AI decisions and auto transitions with timestamps)\n *\n * FRG-209 acceptance: \"Debug page shows: character states, object states,\n * decision log, all in real-time\"\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n\nconst DEBUG_URL = '/debug.html';\n\n/** Wait for the debug.html page to be hydrated with live data */\nasync function waitForDebugPage(page, timeout = 25_000) {\n  await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n  // Wait until the CHARACTERS section is populated with at least one character name\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const text = document.body.innerText;\n      return text.includes('CHARACTERS') && (\n        text.includes('Maple') || text.includes('Sol') || text.includes('Nyx')\n      );\n    },\n    { timeout }\n  );\n}\n\ntest.describe('Regression: Debug dashboard (/debug.html) \u2014 character states and event log', () => {\n  test('debug.html page loads and shows sim clock', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/AI Aquarium/i);\n    // Clock matches HH:MM AM/PM format\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => /\\d+:\\d{2}\\s*(AM|PM)/.test(document.body.innerText),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n    expect(bodyText).toMatch(/\\d+:\\d{2}\\s*(AM|PM)/);\n    console.log('Debug dashboard loaded with sim clock');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html OBJECTS section shows all 10 objects with tile positions', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => document.body.innerText.includes('OBJECTS'),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // OBJECTS heading\n    expect(bodyText).toMatch(/OBJECTS\\s*\\(10\\)/);\n\n    // All 10 object names\n    const expected = ['Stove', 'Fridge', 'Tea Set', 'Kotatsu', 'TV', 'Bookshelf', 'Desk', 'Bed', 'Easel', 'Bonsai'];\n    for (const obj of expected) {\n      expect(bodyText, `\"${obj}\" should appear in debug dashboard OBJECTS section`).toContain(obj);\n    }\n\n    // Tile positions are shown\n    expect(bodyText).toMatch(/tile \\(\\d+, \\d+\\)/);\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section shows all 3 character names in real-time', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(bodyText, 'CHARACTERS section heading missing').toContain('CHARACTERS');\n    expect(bodyText, 'Maple not found in CHARACTERS section').toContain('Maple');\n    expect(bodyText, 'Sol not found in CHARACTERS section').toContain('Sol');\n    expect(bodyText, 'Nyx not found in CHARACTERS section').toContain('Nyx');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section shows action, mood, and energy for each character', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Mood and energy values should appear (characters start at 100)\n    expect(bodyText, 'Mood labels missing from CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/Mood/);\n    expect(bodyText, 'Energy labels missing from CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/Energy/);\n\n    // Action labels\n    expect(bodyText, 'Action labels missing from CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/Action/);\n\n    // Mood/energy values (100 at start)\n    expect(bodyText, 'Expected \"100\" for mood/energy in CHARACTERS').toContain('100');\n\n    console.log('CHARACTERS section contains action, mood, energy \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section shows position and AI next timer', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Position shown as (x, y)\n    expect(bodyText, 'Character position not shown').toMatch(/\\(\\d+, \\d+\\)/);\n\n    // AI next timer (seconds remaining until next decision)\n    expect(bodyText, 'AI next timer not shown in CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/AI next\\s*\\d+s/);\n\n    console.log('CHARACTERS section shows position and AI next timer \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html EVENT LOG shows AI decisions after first cycle', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    // Wait for EVENT LOG section to appear and be populated\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => {\n        const text = document.body.innerText;\n        return text.includes('EVENT LOG') && /AI/.test(text);\n      },\n      { timeout: 20_000 }\n    );\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(bodyText, 'EVENT LOG heading missing').toContain('EVENT LOG');\n    // AI-sourced entries should appear\n    expect(bodyText, 'No AI entries in EVENT LOG').toMatch(/AI/);\n    // Entries include character names\n    const hasCharInLog = /Maple|Sol|Nyx/.test(bodyText);\n    expect(hasCharInLog, 'No character names found in EVENT LOG').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('EVENT LOG contains AI decisions \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html EVENT LOG shows auto state transitions', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(8_000); // allow some auto-transitions\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Auto transitions (e.g. \"idle \u2192 brewing\" or \"auto\" label)\n    // These may appear as \"auto\" source or as idle\u2192active transitions\n    const hasAutoOrTransition = /auto|idle\\s*\u2192|\u2192\\s*idle/.test(bodyText);\n    if (!hasAutoOrTransition) {\n      console.log('No auto transitions observed yet \u2014 may require longer observation window');\n    }\n\n    // EVENT LOG section must exist\n    expect(bodyText, 'EVENT LOG section missing from debug dashboard').toContain('EVENT LOG');\n\n    console.log('EVENT LOG verified \u2014 section exists and is populating');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html object state changes are reflected in real-time in OBJECTS section', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => document.body.innerText.includes('OBJECTS'),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    // Click \"use\" on the bonsai to trigger a state change\n    const useButtons = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'use' });\n    await useButtons.first().click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Some object should now show a non-idle state\n    const hasActiveState = /tending|brewing|cooking|cozy|watching|reading|sleeping|sketching|working|researching/i.test(bodyText);\n    expect(hasActiveState, 'No active object state visible after clicking \"use\" in debug dashboard').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('Debug dashboard reflects real-time object state changes \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section updates when character action changes', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    // Record character actions at t=0\n    const before = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // Wait for movement to potentially change some character state\n    await page.waitForTimeout(5_000);\n    const after = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    // The page content should change (indicating real-time updates)\n    // At minimum the AI next timer countdown should have decremented\n    const changed = before !== after;\n    expect(changed, 'Debug dashboard content did not update over 5s \u2014 page may be static').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('Debug dashboard updates in real-time \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html Pause/Resume controls exist and are functional', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => /Pause|Resume/.test(document.body.innerText),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const pauseBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /Pause|Resume/i });\n    await expect(pauseBtn).toBeVisible();\n\n    // Click pause \u2014 sim clock should stop advancing\n    await pauseBtn.click();\n    const t1 = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText.match(/\\d+:\\d{2}\\s*(AM|PM)/)?.[0]);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(3_000);\n    const t2 = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText.match(/\\d+:\\d{2}\\s*(AM|PM)/)?.[0]);\n\n    console.log(`Sim time before pause-check: ${t1}, after 3s: ${t2}`);\n    // If paused, t1 and t2 should be equal OR the page should show \"Resume\" button\n    const resumeBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /Resume/i });\n    const isPaused = await resumeBtn.isVisible().catch(() => false) ||\n                     (await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Pause/i }).isVisible().catch(() => false));\n    expect(isPaused, 'Pause button or Resume button should be visible').toBe(true);\n  });\n});\n"

page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => document.body.innerText.includes('EVENT LOG'),\n      { timeout: 20_000 }\n    );\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n    expect(bodyText, 'EVENT LOG section missing from debug dashboard').toContain('EVENT LOG');\n\n    // Log may contain auto transitions (idle \u2192 state) or AI decisions (interact \u2192 object)\n    const hasTransitionOrAI = /auto|\u2192|AI/i.test(bodyText);\n    expect(hasTransitionOrAI, 'EVENT LOG appears empty \u2014 no transitions or AI events found').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('EVENT LOG is populated with event entries \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html object \"use\" button triggers state change visible in real-time', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => document.body.innerText.includes('OBJECTS'),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const useButtons = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'use' });\n    await useButtons.first().click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    const hasActiveState = /tending|brewing|cooking|cozy|watching|reading|sleeping|sketching|working|researching/i.test(bodyText);\n    expect(hasActiveState, 'No active object state visible after clicking \"use\" in debug dashboard').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('Debug dashboard reflects real-time object state changes \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section updates over time (real-time)', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const before = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(5_000);\n    const after = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(before !== after, 'Debug dashboard content did not update over 5s \u2014 page may be static').toBe(true);\n    console.log('Debug dashboard updates in real-time \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html Pause/Resume controls exist and are functional', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => /Pause|Resume/.test(document.body.innerText),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const pauseBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /Pause|Resume/i });\n    await expect(pauseBtn).toBeVisible();\n\n    await pauseBtn.click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(1_000);\n\n    // After clicking, either \"Resume\" button is shown or \"Pause\" stays \u2014 either is acceptable\n    const anyCtrlVisible = await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Pause|Resume/i }).isVisible();\n    expect(anyCtrlVisible, 'Pause/Resume control should remain visible after clicking').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('Pause/Resume controls functional \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n});\n"
}

{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01ARRaFNErYpGZGv9FjdC5QN",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/debug-panel-extended.spec.js",
      "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-209\n * Validates the full debug dashboard at /debug.html.\n *\n * The sidebar at / shows only a mini OBJECT STATES panel.\n * The full dashboard at /debug.html shows:\n *   - OBJECTS section (name, state, tile, timer, use button)\n *   - CHARACTERS section (name, action, position, target, mood, energy, AI next timer)\n *   - EVENT LOG (AI decisions and auto transitions with timestamps)\n *\n * FRG-209 acceptance: \"Debug page shows: character states, object states,\n * decision log, all in real-time\"\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n\nconst DEBUG_URL = '/debug.html';\n\n/** Wait for the debug.html page to be hydrated with live data */\nasync function waitForDebugPage(page, timeout = 25_000) {\n  await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const text = document.body.innerText;\n      return text.includes('CHARACTERS') && (\n        text.includes('Maple') || text.includes('Sol') || text.includes('Nyx')\n      );\n    },\n    { timeout }\n  );\n}\n\ntest.describe('Regression: Debug dashboard (/debug.html) \u2014 character states and event log', () => {\n  test('debug.html page loads and shows sim clock', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/AI Aquarium/i);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => /\\d+:\\d{2}\\s*(AM|PM)/.test(document.body.innerText),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n    expect(bodyText).toMatch(/\\d+:\\d{2}\\s*(AM|PM)/);\n    console.log('Debug dashboard loaded with sim clock');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html OBJECTS section shows all 10 objects with tile positions', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => document.body.innerText.includes('OBJECTS'),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(bodyText).toMatch(/OBJECTS\\s*\\(10\\)/);\n\n    const expected = ['Stove', 'Fridge', 'Tea Set', 'Kotatsu', 'TV', 'Bookshelf', 'Desk', 'Bed', 'Easel', 'Bonsai'];\n    for (const obj of expected) {\n      expect(bodyText, `\"${obj}\" should appear in debug dashboard OBJECTS section`).toContain(obj);\n    }\n\n    expect(bodyText).toMatch(/tile \\(\\d+, \\d+\\)/);\n    console.log('All 10 objects with tile positions confirmed in debug dashboard');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section shows all 3 character names in real-time', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(bodyText, 'CHARACTERS section heading missing').toContain('CHARACTERS');\n    expect(bodyText, 'Maple not found in CHARACTERS section').toContain('Maple');\n    expect(bodyText, 'Sol not found in CHARACTERS section').toContain('Sol');\n    expect(bodyText, 'Nyx not found in CHARACTERS section').toContain('Nyx');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section shows action, mood, and energy for each character', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(bodyText, 'Action labels missing from CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/Action/);\n    expect(bodyText, 'Mood labels missing from CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/Mood/);\n    expect(bodyText, 'Energy labels missing from CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/Energy/);\n    expect(bodyText, 'Expected \"100\" for mood/energy in CHARACTERS').toContain('100');\n\n    console.log('CHARACTERS section contains action, mood, energy \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section shows position and AI next timer', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(bodyText, 'Character position not shown').toMatch(/\\(\\d+, \\d+\\)/);\n    expect(bodyText, 'AI next timer not shown in CHARACTERS section').toMatch(/AI next\\s*\\d+s/);\n\n    console.log('CHARACTERS section shows position and AI next timer \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html EVENT LOG shows AI decisions after first cycle', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => {\n        const text = document.body.innerText;\n        return text.includes('EVENT LOG') && /AI/.test(text);\n      },\n      { timeout: 20_000 }\n    );\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(bodyText, 'EVENT LOG heading missing').toContain('EVENT LOG');\n    expect(bodyText, 'No AI entries in EVENT LOG').toMatch(/AI/);\n    const hasCharInLog = /Maple|Sol|Nyx/.test(bodyText);\n    expect(hasCharInLog, 'No character names found in EVENT LOG').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('EVENT LOG contains AI decisions \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html EVENT LOG shows object state transitions', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => document.body.innerText.includes('EVENT LOG'),\n      { timeout: 20_000 }\n    );\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n    expect(bodyText, 'EVENT LOG section missing from debug dashboard').toContain('EVENT LOG');\n\n    // Log may contain auto transitions (idle \u2192 state) or AI decisions (interact \u2192 object)\n    const hasTransitionOrAI = /auto|\u2192|AI/i.test(bodyText);\n    expect(hasTransitionOrAI, 'EVENT LOG appears empty \u2014 no transitions or AI events found').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('EVENT LOG is populated with event entries \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html object \"use\" button triggers state change visible in real-time', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => document.body.innerText.includes('OBJECTS'),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const useButtons = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'use' });\n    await useButtons.first().click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    const hasActiveState = /tending|brewing|cooking|cozy|watching|reading|sleeping|sketching|working|researching/i.test(bodyText);\n    expect(hasActiveState, 'No active object state visible after clicking \"use\" in debug dashboard').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('Debug dashboard reflects real-time object state changes \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html CHARACTERS section updates over time (real-time)', async ({ page }) => {\n    await waitForDebugPage(page);\n\n    const before = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(5_000);\n    const after = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n\n    expect(before !== after, 'Debug dashboard content did not update over 5s \u2014 page may be static').toBe(true);\n    console.log('Debug dashboard updates in real-time \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n\n  test('debug.html Pause/Resume controls exist and are functional', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.goto(DEBUG_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => /Pause|Resume/.test(document.body.innerText),\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const pauseBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /Pause|Resume/i });\n    await expect(pauseBtn).toBeVisible();\n\n    await pauseBtn.click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(1_000);\n\n    // After clicking, either \"Resume\" button is shown or \"Pause\" stays \u2014 either is acceptable\n    const anyCtrlVisible = await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Pause|Resume/i }).isVisible();\n    expect(anyCtrlVisible, 'Pause/Resume control should remain visible after clicking').toBe(true);\n\n    console.log('Pause/Resume controls functional \u2014 confirmed');\n  });\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #102
Two more failures, but for different reasons: 1. **"use" button test fails** - The active state names I'm checking (tending, brewing, cooking, cozy, etc.) don't match what's actually shown. The debug.html probe showed "Tea Set: brewing" and "Kotatsu: cozy" - so these state names DO exist. But the "use" button on debug.html might work differently from the sidebar. Perhaps on debug.html, the buttons don't trigger immediate state changes like the sidebar's "use" buttons do, OR the first available object might already be in a non-idle state so clicking "use" doesn't transition it the same way. Let me check what state the objects are in before clicking. Actually, looking at the debug.html probe output: Tea Set was "brewing" with "15s remaining". So it might already be active. When I click the first "use" button, it might be for an already-active object (Tea Set), and "use" on an active object might not change the state. Or the debug.html buttons might say "use" for all idle objects, so I need to find one that IS idle and click it. 2. **Pause/Resume test fails** - The Pause button may not exist with the text "Pause" - it was shown in the probe as "▮▮ Pause" (pause icon + text). The `getByRole('button', { name: /Pause|Resume/i })` might not match "▮▮ Pause" because of the icon characters interfering with the text matching. Actually, wait - the regex should still match since it's looking for "Pause" anywhere in the name. The real issue is that after clicking the button, it becomes invisible or the page navigates away, which suggests the button might be doing something other than toggling pause state. Let me check what state the page is in when we first load and click - maybe the object is already active, or I need to wait for idle objects to appear before interacting with them. Pause|Resume/i regex should catch both states, so if the test is failing, the button text must be changing to something unexpected or the element is disappearing entirely. Let me look at what actually happens when clicking—either the text transforms into something the regex doesn't match, or the button moves to a different container. I should make these tests more flexible by checking what state names actually appear after interacting with the page, since the initial probe showed "brewing" but that might not persist on a fresh load. Looking at the object state mappings, I can see each object has a specific interaction state it enters—stove goes to "cooking", bonsai to "tending", tea set to "brewing", and so on. My regex pattern includes "brewing", so that should match, but the issue is that in a fresh test run everything starts idle, so clicking the first "use" button would trigger the first object in the list order rather than the tea set. I need to verify what state name the debug.html "use" button actually produces by running a probe on a fresh page load.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

Let me probe the debug.html "use" button and pause button behavior:

    "text": "Let me probe the debug.html \"use\" button and pause button behavior:"
